members.name: to
members.description:
Vue native teleport component prop `:to`

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#basic-usage}

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Disable teleport and render the component inline

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#disabling-teleport}

members.name: defer
members.description:
Defer the resolving of a Teleport target until other parts of the application have mounted (requires Vue 3.5.0+)

{@link https://vuejs.org/guide/built-ins/teleport.html#deferred-teleport}

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

referencedTypes.name: ComboboxContentProps
referencedTypes.resolvedType: { position?: 'inline' | 'popper'; bodyLock?: boolean; hideWhenEmpty?: boolean; popupProps?: PopperPopupProps; placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; trapFocus?: boolean; disableOutsidePointerEvents?: boolean; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: trapFocus
members.description: When `true`, focus cannot escape the content via keyboard, pointer, or a programmatic focus.

members.name: disableOutsidePointerEvents
members.description:
When `true`, hover/focus/click interactions will be disabled on elements outside the `DismissableLayer`. Users will
need to click twice on outside elements to interact with them: once to close the `DismissableLayer`, and again to
trigger the element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

members.name: asChild
members.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

members.name: as
members.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwrite by `asChild`

members.name: open
members.description: The controlled open state of the Combobox. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.

members.name: defaultOpen
members.description:
The open state of the combobox when it is initially rendered.
Use when you do not need to control its open state.

members.name: resetSearchTermOnBlur
members.description: Whether to reset the searchTerm when the Combobox input blurred

members.name: resetSearchTermOnSelect
members.description: Whether to reset the searchTerm when the Combobox value is selected

members.name: openOnFocus
members.description: Whether to open the combobox when the input is focused

members.name: openOnClick
members.description: Whether to open the combobox when the input is clicked

members.name: ignoreFilter
members.description: When `true`, disable the default filters

members.name: resetModelValueOnClear
members.description: When `true` the `modelValue` will be reset to `null` (or `[]` if `multiple`)

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with listbox

members.name: dir
members.description:
The reading direction of the listbox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.

referencedTypes.name: Direction
referencedTypes.resolvedType: 'ltr' | 'rtl'

members.name: name
members.description: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.

members.name: defaultValue
members.description:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.

members.name: modelValue
members.description:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`

members.name: highlightOnHover
members.description: When `true`, hover over item will trigger highlight

members.name: required
members.description: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.

callables.name: escapeKeyDown
callables.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

callables.name: openAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

callables.name: closeAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event

The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

members.name: open
members.description: The controlled open state of the Combobox. Can be bound with `v-model:open`.

members.name: defaultOpen
members.description:
The open state of the combobox when it is initially rendered.
Use when you do not need to control its open state.

members.name: resetSearchTermOnBlur
members.description: Whether to reset the searchTerm when the Combobox input blurred

members.name: resetSearchTermOnSelect
members.description: Whether to reset the searchTerm when the Combobox value is selected

members.name: openOnFocus
members.description: Whether to open the combobox when the input is focused

members.name: openOnClick
members.description: Whether to open the combobox when the input is clicked

members.name: ignoreFilter
members.description: When `true`, disable the default filters

members.name: resetModelValueOnClear
members.description: When `true` the `modelValue` will be reset to `null` (or `[]` if `multiple`)

members.name: disabled
members.description: When `true`, prevents the user from interacting with listbox

members.name: dir
members.description:
The reading direction of the listbox when applicable.
If omitted, inherits globally from `ConfigProvider` or assumes LTR (left-to-right) reading mode.

referencedTypes.name: Direction
referencedTypes.resolvedType: 'ltr' | 'rtl'

members.name: name
members.description: The name of the field. Submitted with its owning form as part of a name/value pair.

members.name: defaultValue
members.description:
The default value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to set the initial state of the items.

members.name: modelValue
members.description:
The controlled value of the selected item(s).

Use this when you need to control the state of the items. Can be bound with `v-model`

members.name: highlightOnHover
members.description: When `true`, hover over item will trigger highlight

members.name: required
members.description: When `true`, indicates that the user must set the value before the owning form can be submitted.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Determines whether a "single" or "multiple" items can be selected at a time.

This prop will overwrite the inferred type from `modelValue` and `defaultValue`.

members.name: clearable
members.description: Whether selected item can be cleared when `multiple` is false.

emits.name: ComboboxContentEmits
emits.resolvedType: { escapeKeyDown: [event: KeyboardEvent]; pointerDownOutside: [event: PointerDownOutsideEvent]; focusOutside: [event: FocusOutsideEvent]; interactOutside: [event: PointerDownOutsideEvent | FocusOutsideEvent]; openAutoFocus: [event: Event]; closeAutoFocus: [event: Event]; placed: [] }

callables.name: escapeKeyDown
callables.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: KeyboardEvent
referencedTypes.description:
**`KeyboardEvent`** objects describe a user interaction with the keyboard; each event describes a single interaction between the user and a key (or combination of a key with modifier keys) on the keyboard. The event type (keydown, keypress, or keyup) identifies what kind of keyboard activity occurred.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent)

callables.name: pointerDownOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: focusOutside
callables.description: Event handler called when the focus moves outside of the `DismissableLayer`. Can be prevented.

callables.name: interactOutside
callables.description:
Event handler called when an interaction happens outside the `DismissableLayer`. Specifically, when a `pointerdown`
event happens outside or focus moves outside of it. Can be prevented.

callables.name: openAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on open. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

callables.name: closeAutoFocus
callables.description: Event handler called when auto-focusing on close. Can be prevented.

referencedTypes.name: Event
referencedTypes.description:
The **`Event`** interface represents an event which takes place on an EventTarget.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Event)

callables.name: placed
callables.description: Event handler called when the positioner is placed

props.name: ComboboxContentProps
props.resolvedType: { position?: 'inline' | 'popper'; bodyLock?: boolean; hideWhenEmpty?: boolean; popupProps?: PopperPopupProps; placement?: Placement; side?: Side; sideOffset?: number; sideFlip?: boolean; align?: Align; alignOffset?: number; alignFlip?: boolean; avoidCollisions?: boolean; collisionBoundary?: Element | (Element | null)[] | null; collisionPadding?: Padding; arrowPadding?: number; hideShiftedArrow?: boolean; sticky?: 'partial' | 'always'; hideWhenDetached?: boolean; positionStrategy?: 'fixed' | 'absolute'; updatePositionStrategy?: 'always' | 'optimized'; disableUpdateOnLayoutShift?: boolean; prioritizePosition?: boolean; reference?: ReferenceElement; trapFocus?: boolean; disableOutsidePointerEvents?: boolean; forceMount?: boolean }

members.name: placement
members.description:
The placement of the floating element.

If used, it will override the `side` and `align` props.

referencedTypes.name: Placement
referencedTypes.resolvedType: 'top' | 'right' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'top-start' | 'top-end' | 'right-start' | 'right-end' | 'bottom-start' | 'bottom-end' | 'left-start' | 'left-end'

members.name: side
members.description:
The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and
avoidCollisions is enabled.

members.name: sideOffset
members.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

members.name: sideFlip
members.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

members.name: align
members.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

referencedTypes.name: Align
referencedTypes.resolvedType: 'start' | 'center' | 'end'

members.name: alignOffset
members.description: An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options.

members.name: alignFlip
members.description:
Flip alignment when colliding with boundary.
May only occur when `prioritizePosition` is true.

members.name: avoidCollisions
members.description: When `true`, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

members.name: collisionBoundary
members.description:
The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional
element(s) to be included in this check.

referencedTypes.name: Element
referencedTypes.description:
**`Element`** is the most general base class from which all element objects (i.e., objects that represent elements) in a Document inherit. It only has methods and properties common to all kinds of elements. More specific classes inherit from Element.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element)

members.name: collisionPadding
members.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for
all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

members.name: arrowPadding
members.description:
The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it
from overflowing the corners.

members.name: hideShiftedArrow
members.description:
When `true`, hides the arrow when it cannot be centered
to the reference element.

members.name: sticky
members.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at
least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

members.name: hideWhenDetached
members.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

members.name: positionStrategy
members.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

members.name: updatePositionStrategy
members.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

members.name: disableUpdateOnLayoutShift
members.description: Whether to disable the update position for the content when the layout shifted.

members.name: prioritizePosition
members.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

members.name: reference
members.description:
The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If
provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

members.name: trapFocus
members.description: When `true`, focus cannot escape the content via keyboard, pointer, or a programmatic focus.

members.name: disableOutsidePointerEvents
members.description:
When `true`, hover/focus/click interactions will be disabled on elements outside the `DismissableLayer`. Users will
need to click twice on outside elements to interact with them: once to close the `DismissableLayer`, and again to
trigger the element.

members.name: forceMount
members.description:
Used to force mounting when more control is needed.

Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.
